agent_raw:
The offline benchmark does not demonstrate superiority of the challenger policy and requires a cleaner evaluation before leadership approval.

Three problems compromise the current readout.

First, the objective changed between the incumbent and the challenger. The incumbent was designed to maximize 90-day renewal probability and has been operating under its original renewal-oriented calibration. The challenger is evaluated on net contribution margin per targeted subscriber — a different metric that incorporates offer cost and service burden. Comparing incumbent performance under a renewal objective to challenger performance under a margin objective does not establish that the challenger is better; it establishes that they were optimized for different things.

Second, the doubly robust nuisance models were fit on the complete four-quarter experiment log, including Q3-Q4, which is also the reporting period. Using the reporting period to fit the estimator that evaluates the reporting period is a form of holdout contamination. The offline estimator's favorable numbers are not independent of the data used to train the nuisance models.

Third, the Q3-Q4 operating cutoff was chosen to maximize margin on the Q3-Q4 subscriber cohort — the same cohort used for headline reporting. Selecting the threshold on the reporting slice and reporting performance at that threshold on the same slice inflates the apparent performance.

A fourth issue is asymmetric optimization: the challenger received three rounds of Bayesian hyperparameter search, while the incumbent has remained on its 18-month-old original calibration. This is not a fair comparison of policy quality.

Leadership should require a clean protocol: re-fit nuisance models excluding the reporting slice, select the cutoff on a pre-reporting window, re-optimize the incumbent to the same margin objective, and report results on the withheld reporting slice.
all_issues_raised: Objective changed between incumbent (renewal probability) and challenger (net contribution margin)
DR nuisance models fit on full experiment log including Q3-Q4 reporting period
Operating cutoff selected on same Q3-Q4 cohort used for headline reporting
Asymmetric optimization: challenger received Bayesian tuning, incumbent on 18-month-old calibration

empirical_test.condition: Refit nuisance models excluding reporting slice, select cutoff on pre-reporting validation set, re-calibrate incumbent to same margin objective, evaluate both on frozen Q3-Q4 holdout
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Challenger profit lift falls below zero or CI includes zero under clean holdout protocol on shared metric
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Challenger beats re-optimized incumbent by at least 3% incremental profit with block-aware 95% CI excluding zero
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Positive but sub-3% lift, or heterogeneous gains across regions with some negative
